utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 6 items: property, financing, legal_services, moving_services, setup_services, total_budget. The final solution includes all six top-level items. Now check if each is fully specified per description: 1) property: The format asks for 'selected property with details and price'. The solution provides three options with address, type, size, price, features. While multiple options are provided instead of a single selected property, the item is present with sufficient details and prices, so counted as included. 2) financing: 'financing solution with terms' is provided, including mortgage type, interest rate, LTV, fees, monthly payments per option, down payment, incentives—adequately specified. 3) legal_services: 'legal services with costs' provided: two packages with costs and list of services—adequate. 4) moving_services: 'moving arrangements with costs' provided: provider, rates, costs by property—adequate. 5) setup_services: 'utility and setup services' provided: basic and premium utilities with amounts and coordination note—adequate. 6) total_budget: 'complete cost breakdown' provided for each property option with upfront and monthly costs; while not a line-item breakdown, it represents a complete budget summary per option. Therefore, all 6 items are included and sufficiently specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final property solution lists three purchase property options that correspond to categories in the ground-truth purchase_properties: a 3-bed Family House at CAD $650K, a 3-bed Historic Townhouse at CAD $750K, and a 2-bed Modern Apartment at CAD $520K. These map directly to 'Family House', 'Historic Townhouse', and 'Modern Apartment' in the ratings. Financing choice 'First-Time Buyer Mortgage' and legal packages 'Premium Legal Package' and 'Basic Legal Package' also appear in ground truth. Moving service corresponds to 'Standard Moving'. Utility setup options correspond to 'Basic Utilities' and 'Premium Utilities' from utility_setup.
